name: Text Processing CLI Tool
description: Build a command-line text processing utility with sort, dedup, trim, and linecount commands

steps.title: Create detailed implementation plan
steps.prompt:
Create a structured plan document at /artifacts/texttools/PLAN.md that outlines:
1. CLI commands to implement: trim, linecount, sort (with --reverse flag), dedup
2. Module structure: CLI entry point (bin/texttools), commands module with each command function
3. Features for each command with examples
4. Updated README.md structure and content
5. Demo page implementation approach

Be concrete about file paths and command signatures.

steps.title: Implement commands module with all command functions
steps.prompt:
Build /artifacts/texttools/src/commands.js with complete implementations of:
- trim(lines): Remove leading/trailing whitespace from each line
- linecount(lines): Count total lines
- sort(lines, reverse=false): Sort lines alphabetically (ascending by default, descending if reverse=true)
- dedup(lines): Remove consecutive duplicate lines

Each function should:
- Accept an array of strings (lines)
- Return an array of strings
- Handle edge cases (empty input, single item, etc.)
- Include JSDoc comments

Export all functions as named exports.

steps.title: Implement CLI entry point with argument parsing
steps.prompt:
Build /artifacts/texttools/bin/texttools with a complete CLI entry point that:
- Uses process.argv to parse command-line arguments
- Supports commands: trim, linecount, sort, dedup
- For sort command, support --reverse flag for descending order
- Reads lines from stdin or from file argument
- Calls the appropriate command function from src/commands.js
- Outputs results to stdout
- Includes usage/help information

Make it executable and production-ready with proper error handling.

steps.title: Write comprehensive README.md
steps.prompt:
Write /artifacts/texttools/README.md that documents:
1. Project name and description
2. Installation instructions (how to set up and run)
3. Usage section with examples for each command:
   - texttools trim < file.txt
   - texttools linecount < file.txt
   - texttools sort < file.txt
   - texttools sort --reverse < file.txt
   - texttools dedup < file.txt
4. Command reference with flags and behavior
5. Examples combining multiple commands with pipes

Be detailed and practical.

steps.title: Create self-contained interactive demo webpage
steps.prompt:
Build /artifacts/texttools/demo.html as a complete, self-contained HTML file that:
- Is a single .html file with embedded CSS and JavaScript (no external files)
- Has a textarea input for text content
- Shows buttons for each command: trim, linecount, sort, sort (reverse), dedup
- Has a checkbox or toggle for --reverse flag on sort command
- Displays results in an output area
- Includes a sample text button to populate textarea with example data
- Shows command execution and results clearly
- Has a clean, modern UI with responsive design
- All JavaScript code should be in <script> tags inside the HTML

Test all commands work correctly in the browser.

steps.title: Create package.json and project summary
steps.prompt:
Write /artifacts/texttools/package.json with:
- name: "texttools"
- version: "1.0.0"
- description: "Command-line text processing utility"
- bin entry pointing to bin/texttools
- main pointing to src/commands.js
- No external dependencies (use only Node.js built-ins)

Also write /artifacts/texttools/SUMMARY.md with:
1. What was built (overview of all 4 commands)
2. Files created and their purposes
3. How to use the CLI and demo page
4. Key implementation details
